name: Ledge Climb
description: A recovery transition that moves a hanging character onto the top of a ledge using clearance checks, animation alignment, and collision restoration.
design_notes: Ledge climb is the commit step after a grab. Define ledge_id, climb animation, top clearance, root-motion policy, collision disable/enable timing, cancel rules, and fallback behavior when the landing area becomes blocked. The climb should feel reliable even when the ledge belongs to a moving platform or multiplayer authority corrects the character.
design_purpose: Complete a ledge recovery by moving the player from hang state to safe standing or crouched ground.
Let designers author vertical routes that recover from imperfect jumps.
Coordinate animation, collision, camera, and input lock during a short committed traversal transition.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can pull themselves up from danger into a stable position.

parameters.name: ledge_climb_animation_id
parameters.description: Animation or motion profile used for the climb transition.

parameters.name: ledge_top_clearance_units
parameters.description: Clear vertical space required at the landing point.

parameters.name: ledge_climb_window_ms
parameters.description: Committed transition interval from hang to grounded state.

parameters.name: ledge_exit_position_offset
parameters.description: Placement offset applied when the climb finishes.

parameters.name: root_motion_policy_id
parameters.description: Owner of character displacement during the climb.

edge_cases: The top landing area becomes blocked after the player starts climbing.
Damage, knockback, or a scripted event interrupts during root motion and must restore collision safely.
Moving ledges require exit offset relative to moving_platform_id rather than a world point.
Networked play needs correction if client animation reaches the top before server validation.
common_bugs: Collision is disabled for the animation and never restored after cancel.
Root motion moves through the wall because no sweep validates the climb path.
Top clearance uses standing height even when the intended exit is crouched under an obstacle.
Camera snaps to grounded follow before the character exits hang state.
balancing_notes: Long climb animations create vulnerability and tension; short climbs make ledge recovery nearly automatic.
Combat encounters near ledges need clear rules for whether climbing grants protection or vulnerability.
Generous climb acceptance should be paired with clear ledge art so players know valid recovery points.
accessibility_notes: Allow buffered climb input from hang state and avoid requiring rapid repeated presses to recover.
Use camera framing and prompts that remain readable while the character is hanging.

implementation_notes.unity: Validate top clearance before starting and again near exit. If animation root motion moves the character, sweep the capsule along the path and keep platforming_state_id as the authority for collision restoration.
implementation_notes.godot: Use a climb state with stored ledge id, exit transform, and remaining ticks. Move the character through scripted positions in _physics_process unless animation is strictly visual, then restore collision after exit validation.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Motion Warping, root motion, or a custom movement mode with a validated ledge target. Server should approve the climb start and final placement, while clients reconcile if montage timing diverges.
implementation_notes.web: Represent ledge climb as a deterministic transition from hang to exit position. Collision sweeps and top clearance checks run in fixed ticks before render animation advances.

relationships.reason: Ledge climb needs an established grab point, hang state, and clearance check before moving the character up.
Ledge climb is a slower or more explicit variant of mantling with stronger animation and clearance requirements.
Some climb systems spend stamina during the climb to limit repeated ledge recovery.

example_games.title: Uncharted 4
example_games.note: Uses ledge climb transitions in authored traversal routes.

example_games.title: Hollow Knight
example_games.note: Uses ledge-style recovery and climb-adjacent movement in platforming spaces.

example_games.title: Super Mario Odyssey
example_games.note: Uses ledge grabs and climb-up recovery in 3D traversal spaces.
